summary: Clean soundboard recording with decent audio, but an average‑to‑weak 1995 show that shines on Playing > UJB and The Last Time while Jerry’s guitar is often off‑kilter.
review: The GD‑95‑02‑20 tape captures a very clean soundboard mix (minor buzz and occasional venue echo aside) that most listeners praise. Musically the concert sits in the middle of the band’s mid‑90s era – the second set delivers a solid Playing > UJB and The Last Time, but many note Jerry’s playing slipping on songs like El Paso and Bertha, and overall the show feels average with a few standout moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised (≈4/5) while show performance receives mixed to below‑average scores (≈2.8/5). Weighted combination yields a modest overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clean mix, crisp instrument separation; occasional buzz noted, and a few reviewers complained about venue acoustics (roof echo) but the tape itself is free of major drop‑outs or distortion.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Uncle John's Band
show_quality.setlist_flow: Set 1 is uneven, with a few lackluster moments; Set 2 picks up thanks to the strong Playing > UJB sequence and The Last Time, but overall the concert feels like an average mid‑90s performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Frequently described as out of sync, losing his way in jams (Cassidy, El Paso) and sounding ‘off’ or ‘scratching’; a few fans still express love for him despite the decline.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised on the Rain performance and noted as the strongest core member during this era.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mixed remarks – some note his mix being too loud; occasional criticism of leadership in jams, but no strong positive comments.
band_member_comments.Keys: References to Vince, Brent (and “ultra‑cheese” keyboards) are largely negative, calling the keyboard contributions lame or poorly mixed.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally praised; drums and post‑drum jam described as fun and well‑balanced.
